2604.01206.title: LLM REgression with a Latent Iterative State Head
2604.01206.paper_summary: We present RELISH (REgression with a Latent Iterative State Head), a novel, lightweight architecture designed for text regression with large language models. Rather than decoding numeric targets as text or aggregating multiple generated outputs, RELISH predicts scalar values directly from frozen LLM representations by iteratively refining a learned latent state through cross-attention over token-level representations, and then mapping the final state to a point estimate with a linear regressor. Across five datasets, four LLM backbones, and two LLM training regimes, RELISH consistently outperforms prior baselines from all three major LLM regression families, including autoregressive decoding, regression-aware inference, and existing predictive head methods. Despite these gains, RELISH remains highly parameter-efficient, requiring only 3.4-3.7M trainable parameters across frozen LLM backbones (only 0.01-0.04% additional overhead), far less than LoRA-based alternatives that grow with model size (0.26-0.42%).
2604.01206.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 RELISH（REgression with a Latent Iterative State Head），這是一種新穎且輕量的架構，旨在利用大型語言模型進行文本回歸。RELISH 不是將數值目標解碼為文本或聚合多個生成的輸出，而是通過在標記級表示上進行交叉注意力，迭代地細化學習的潛在狀態，直接從凍結的 LLM 表示中預測標量值，然後將最終狀態映射到線性回歸器的點估計。 在五個數據集、四個 LLM 骨幹和兩種 LLM 訓練模式中，RELISH 始終超越來自所有三個主要 LLM 回歸家族的先前基準，包括自回歸解碼、回歸感知推斷和現有的預測頭方法。 儘管取得了這些增益，RELISH 仍然保持高度的參數效率，在凍結的 LLM 骨幹中僅需 3.4-3.7M 的可訓練參數（僅 0.01-0.04% 的額外開銷），遠低於隨模型大小增長的基於 LoRA 的替代方案（0.26-0.42%）。
2604.01206.author: Yiheng Su et.al.
